title: Props
content:
### Props Props (short for properties) are read-only inputs passed to components. They allow data to flow from parent to child. --- ### Passing Props ```jsx function User({ name, age, isAdmin }) { return (

Name: {name}

Age: {age}

{isAdmin && Admin}
); } ``` ### Default Props ```jsx function Button({ label = 'Click', variant = 'primary' }) { return ; } ``` ### Props Are Read-Only Components must never modify their own props. React enforces a strict unidirectional data flow. | Concept | Description | |---------|-------------| | Props | Data passed from parent to child | | Immutable | Cannot be changed by the receiving component | | Any type | Strings, numbers, objects, arrays, functions, JSX | | Children | Special prop for nested content | ### The Children Prop ```jsx function Card({ children }) { return
{children}
; }

Title

Content

 ``` > **Key Point:** Props are the primary mechanism for component communication. Data always flows downward.

quiz.question: Can a component modify its own props?
What special prop is used for nested content?
